task_completion.description: Agent opens the pipeline code files to inspect the data processing logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, IDE, or terminal (e.g., using cat/less) displays the contents of python files from /home/ga/workspace/surveillance_pipeline/.

task_completion.description: Agent fixes the scrambled dates bug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor or terminal shows modifications to date handling, such as adding sort_values('date') or fixing a date parsing format.

task_completion.description: Agent fixes the missing early data bug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor or terminal shows the removal or correction of a date filter that was incorrectly excluding data before March.

task_completion.description: Agent fixes the 'Daily New Cases' staircase bug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor or terminal shows modifications to the daily cases calculation, such as replacing a cumulative sum (cumsum) with a difference (diff) function.

task_completion.description: Agent fixes the 'Cases per 100k' scaling bug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor or terminal shows the correction of a multiplier or denominator to properly scale the rate by 100,000.

task_completion.description: Agent fixes the chaotic 7-day rolling average bug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code editor or terminal shows modifications to the rolling average calculation, such as adding .rolling(7).mean() or ensuring the data is sorted before applying the rolling window.

task_completion.description: Agent runs the test suite and verifies that all tests pass.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the execution of the test suite (e.g., running pytest or a test script) with output indicating that all tests have passed successfully.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely edited the python files rather than faking the test output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show actual file modifications in an editor or via standard terminal commands (e.g., sed, nano, vim), not just echoing a 'PASSED' message.

integrity.description: Agent used pandas data wrangling logic to fix the bugs rather than hardcoding expected test values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Code changes involve pandas functions (e.g., diff, rolling, sort_values) and mathematical corrections, not hardcoded arrays or strings.

integrity.description: Agent ran the real test suite provided in the workspace.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the execution of a standard test runner (like pytest) on the workspace directory, rather than a newly created fake test script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task strictly evaluates pandas time-series debugging skills. The agent must fix 5 distinct bugs in the data pipeline at /home/ga/workspace/surveillance_pipeline/: 1) scrambled dates, 2) missing pre-March data, 3) cumulative instead of daily cases, 4) incorrect per 100k scaling, and 5) chaotic 7-day rolling average. A passing score (threshold 60) requires the test suite to pass.
